title: Elevated Serum Concentrations of Lacosamide and Perampanel in a Pediatric Patient Following Coadministration of Clarithromycin for
abstract: Lacosamide (LCM) and perampanel (PER) are antiepileptic drugs eliminated, in part, by cytochrome P450 (CYP) 3A4. However, clinical reports of their interactions are limited. Here, we report the case of a 12-year-old girl with a history of infantile epileptic spasms syndrome, treated with LCM, PER, valproic acid (VPA), and levetiracetam (LEV), who was diagnosed with
journal: The journal of pediatric pharmacology and therapeutics : JPPT : the official journal of PPAG
